summary: Kenneth Suna presents a hierarchical approach to building an investment portfolio for buy-and-hold investors, recommending S&P 500 index funds as the foundation, followed by tech ETFs, dividend ETFs, and REITs. The video emphasizes researching individual stocks of companies you know, use, and love, using historical examples (Chipotle, Apple, Meta, ELF) to illustrate long-term wealth creation potential.
takeaways: Start with S&P 500 index fund as primary investment
Add tech-based ETF as second investment
Add dividend-based ETF as third investment
Add REIT (real estate investment trust) as fourth investment
Research individual stocks only from companies you know, use, and love
Use fundamental analysis resources and stock comparison templates before investing
title: If I could only buy 1/2/3/4 investments …
